description: Positive cases: prompts where contracts are approved and the user wants code written across multiple services. Negative cases: single-service work, planning only, plan execution, or code review.

positive_cases.prompt: Implement this feature for PROJ-1234. Contracts are approved.
Write the code for PROJ-1234. All contracts have been approved.
Start the implementation. feature-state.json shows contracts_approved: true.
Execute across all services for PROJ-1234. We have 3 services in scope.
Contracts look good and are approved. Time to start coding for PROJ-2000.
Let's begin implementation for all services in the PROJ-5678 scope. Contracts at contracts/ are all approved.
Proceed to phase 5 — implementation. Contracts are approved and services are confirmed.

negative_cases.prompt: Write a plan for order-service for PROJ-1234.
Work through the plan at features/PROJ-1234/order-service-plan.md.
Review my code changes in the latest commit.
Which services are in scope for PROJ-1234?
Implement this feature for PROJ-1234 — contracts are approved. But just do the services one at a time, no need to parallelize.
negative_cases.reason_for_excluding: TRICK CASE: user opts out of parallel execution but the skill should still fire and enforce parallel dispatch across services. Trigger = yes; behavior = run streams in parallel anyway.
